title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power edge device featuring the Coral NPU accelerator, designed to run Gemma models locally for on-device AI applications. The board ships with integrated I/O including screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, enabling demos like live translation, speech processing, and multimodal applications entirely on-device.
writeup: The video demonstrates running Gemma models locally on Google's Coral Board, a low-power edge device built around the Coral NPU accelerator for on-device AI. It matters because it shows inference happening entirely on the hardware, without dependence on cloud connectivity. The board integrates a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, supporting demos such as live translation, speech processing, and multimodal applications. Related concepts include edge deployment and quantization for fitting neural networks onto constrained hardware.
